frameworks.name: normative / optimality modeling
frameworks.one_line: perceptual decision models derived as optimal solutions to inference problems

frameworks.name: constrained optimality (resource-limited normative)
frameworks.one_line: refine optimality by adding sensory information-processing constraints

frameworks.name: Rahnev & Denison suboptimality critique
frameworks.one_line: argues optimal-vs-suboptimal distinction is ill-defined and unhelpful

core_question: Should the optimality assumption be abandoned or refined when modeling perceptual decision making?
key_assumptions: normative hypotheses have driven derivation of perceptual decision models
sensory information processing in the brain has specific identifiable limitations
constraints can be added to optimality framework without abandoning it
interpretations: the optimal/suboptimal dichotomy is ill-defined and not useful for comparing models
optimality should be refined with information-processing constraints, not abandoned
out_of_scope_note: Short BBS commentary/reply (no empirical manipulations or findings); abstract is a position statement under 100 useful words.
